bingo_tasks.name: Task Bingo
bingo_tasks.description: Everyone gets a bingo card with fun tasks to complete.
bingo_tasks.instructions:
**Preparation:**
Create 5x5 bingo cards with 25 different tasks. Examples: 'Give a stranger a compliment', 'Photo with a police officer', 'Sing a song'. Each participant gets a card and a pen.

**Gameplay:**
The group goes through the city. Everyone tries to complete tasks from their card. Completed squares are checked off. Fellow players can serve as witnesses.

**Rules:**
• First row (horizontal, vertical, or diagonal) = 'BINGO!'
• All 25 squares = 'Super-BINGO!'
• Each task must be documented with a photo
• No task can be skipped

**Variants:**
• Team Bingo: Two teams compete against each other
• Drinking Bingo: Toast for each completed square
• Speed Bingo: 2-hour time limit

**Tips:**
Mix easy and difficult tasks. The best cards have a good balance between embarrassing and doable tasks!

drink_roulette.name: Drink Roulette
drink_roulette.description: The wheel of fortune decides who drinks.
drink_roulette.instructions:
**Preparation:**
Get a roulette wheel or create one with names/numbers. Set up drinks (shots, beer, or non-alcoholic). Each player gets a number or position assigned.

**Gameplay:**
Taking turns, each player spins the wheel. Whoever the pointer lands on must drink. The spinner can also assign an additional task.

**Rules:**
• Hit twice in a row: Double shot
• Pointer lands on yourself: Everyone else drinks
• 'Joker' field: Player chooses who drinks

**Variants:**
• Task Roulette: Tasks instead of drinking
• Challenge Roulette: Fields with different challenges
• Truth Roulette: Whoever gets hit must answer a question honestly

**Tips:**
For more excitement: Write special actions on some fields like 'Everyone drinks' or 'Waterfall'!

who_am_i.name: Who Am I?
who_am_i.description: Guess the famous person on your forehead through yes/no questions.
who_am_i.instructions:
**Preparation:**
Write names of famous people on sticky notes - for bachelor parties, ideally celebrity couples or theme-related names. Everyone gets a note stuck to their forehead without seeing it.

**Gameplay:**
Taking turns, everyone asks a yes/no question ('Am I female?', 'Am I an actor?'). If 'yes', you can keep asking. If 'no', it's the next person's turn.

**Rules:**
• Only yes/no questions allowed
• Correct guess: New note or you're out
• Maximum 20 questions per round
• No hints from others!

**Variants:**
• Couples Edition: Guess famous couples
• Movie Edition: Only movie characters
• Bachelor Party Special: Exes of the groom/bride

**Tips:**
With large groups, play in teams. Question tips: First clarify gender, then profession, then era!

flunkyball.name: Flunkyball
flunkyball.description: The ultimate outdoor drinking game with ball and bottle.
flunkyball.instructions:
**Preparation:**
Two teams line up about 10 meters apart. Everyone has a drink. In the middle stands a bottle partially filled with water. A soft ball (tennis/softball) is needed.

**Gameplay:**
Team A throws the ball at the bottle. If hit: Team A drinks quickly. Team B must set up the bottle, retrieve the ball, and line up. Then Team A is stopped.

**Rules:**
• Ball must be thrown while sitting
• Only put down drink when all team members are standing
• Bottle must stand upright (not leaning)
• Whoever finishes drinking places bottle upside down
• First team completely finished wins

**Variants:**
• Relay: Only one person per team throws
• Hardcore: Every hit = open a new drink
• Softcore: Water instead of alcohol

**Tips:**
Perfect for parks or gardens! The bottle shouldn't tip over too easily. Sunshine makes the game even better!

beer_pong.name: Beer Pong
beer_pong.description: The classic - throw the ball into your opponent's cups.
beer_pong.instructions:
**Preparation:**
Set up 10 cups per team in pyramid formation (4-3-2-1) on both sides of a long table. Fill each cup one-third with beer. Prepare water cups for cleaning the balls.

**Gameplay:**
Two teams face each other. Taking turns, each player throws a ping pong ball. If it lands in a cup, the opposing team drinks and removes the cup.

**Rules:**
• 'On Fire': 2+ hits in a row = keep throwing
• 'Elbow Rule': Elbow behind table edge
• 'Re-Rack': 1-2 reformations allowed (at 4 or 2 cups)
• Both players hit = balls back
• Opponent may block 'Bounce'

**Variants:**
• Death Cup: Hit into cup still being drunk = instant win
• Bounce Shot: Bounced ball counts double, but can be blocked
• Redemption: Loser can keep throwing until they miss

**Tips:**
The perfect throw has a slight arc. For non-alcoholic version: Water in cups, shots on the side!

groom_bride_quiz.name: Groom/Bride Quiz
groom_bride_quiz.description: How well does the group know the guest of honor?
groom_bride_quiz.instructions:
**Preparation:**
Before the party, collect 20-30 questions about the guest of honor: Favorite things, embarrassing stories, relationship details. The partner can be secretly interviewed. Note the answers!

**Gameplay:**
The moderator asks questions. The group (or the guest of honor) must answer. Wrong answers result in penalties for the guest of honor or the group.

**Rules:**
• Wrong answer = Shot/task for guest of honor
• Correct answer = Shot/task for the group
• Joker: Partner can be called once
• Intimate questions: Guest of honor may refuse (= penalty)

**Variants:**
• Team Quiz: Group vs. guest of honor
• Speed Quiz: 10 seconds per answer
• Photo Quiz: Show pictures from the past

**Tips:**
Mix funny, embarrassing, and romantic questions. The best questions often come from the partner! Document everything for the wedding newspaper!

truth_or_dare.name: Truth or Dare
truth_or_dare.description: The classic for brave confessions and crazy tasks.
truth_or_dare.instructions:
**Preparation:**
No special preparation needed, but prepared questions/tasks make it easier. A spinning bottle is optional. Drinks for refusers should be ready.

**Gameplay:**
Taking turns, everyone chooses: 'Truth' (answer a question honestly) or 'Dare' (complete a task). The previous player or the group asks the question/task.

**Rules:**
• Refusal = Penalty (shot, embarrassing task)
• Can switch once per game
• Truth must be honest (group judges)
• Tasks must not endanger anyone

**Variants:**
• Bachelor Party Edition: All questions/tasks about relationships
• Hardcore: Only dare, no truth
• Dice Version: Even = truth, Odd = dare

**Tips:**
Start harmless and escalate slowly. The best dares are embarrassing but safe. Respect boundaries!

city_rally.name: City Rally
city_rally.description: A scavenger hunt through the city with tasks and puzzles.
city_rally.instructions:
**Preparation:**
Plan a route through the city with 8-12 stations. Create clues leading to the next station. Prepare tasks for each station. Form teams (2-4 people).

**Gameplay:**
Teams start staggered. At each station: Read clue, complete task, take photo, find next clue. The fastest team with all tasks wins.

**Rules:**
• All tasks must be photographed
• Time limit: 2-3 hours
• GPS tracking or calls to game master allowed
• Points for speed + creativity

**Variants:**
• Pub Rally: Stations are bars/pubs
• Photo Rally: Creative photos instead of tasks
• QR Code Rally: Clues via QR code

**Tips:**
Use landmarks as stations. The best rally has a mix of knowledge, action, and fun. End at a bar for the awards ceremony!

never_have_i_ever.name: Never Have I Ever
never_have_i_ever.instructions:
**Preparation:**
All players sit in a circle. Everyone has a drink or 10 fingers/chips. Optional: Prepared statements for the start.

**Gameplay:**
Taking turns, everyone says 'Never have I ever...' and adds a statement. Anyone who has done it drinks a sip or loses a finger/chip.

**Rules:**
• Honesty is mandatory!
• At 0 fingers: Must skip a round or take a special shot
• Follow-up questions allowed: 'Really? Tell us!'
• Caught lying: Penalty drink

**Variants:**
• Bachelor Party Edition: Only relationship/dating topics
• Speed Round: 5 seconds per statement
• Story Mode: Whoever drinks must tell the story

**Tips:**
Start harmless and escalate. The best statements are specific enough to be interesting but not too personal. Respect boundaries!

toilet_paper_dress.name: Toilet Paper Wedding Dress
toilet_paper_dress.description: Create a wedding dress using only toilet paper.
toilet_paper_dress.instructions:
**Preparation:**
Divide the group into teams of 3-4 people. Each team gets 2-3 rolls of toilet paper. Optional: Tape, scissors (makes it easier).

**Gameplay:**
Each team chooses a 'model'. In 10-15 minutes, a wedding dress must be created from toilet paper. The model presents the result on a 'runway'.

**Rules:**
• Strict time limit
• Only provided materials
• Model may not help
• Dress must survive a 'catwalk'

**Variants:**
• With Veil: Bonus for accessories
• Fashion Show: With music and commentator
• Jury Rating: Guest of honor rates

**Tips:**
Wrap the dress around the body rather than just hanging! The best dresses have structure (draping, folds). Don't forget photos!

shoe_game.name: Shoe Game
shoe_game.description: Bride and groom answer questions with shoes.
shoe_game.instructions:
**Preparation:**
The couple sits back to back on chairs. Each holds one of their own shoes and one of their partner's shoes. Moderator has a list of 20-30 questions.

**Gameplay:**
The moderator asks questions ('Who's the better cook?'). The couple raises the shoe of the person the answer applies to - without coordinating.

**Rules:**
• No coordination allowed!
• Same answer: Kiss
• Different answer: Brief explanation
• 3 wrong questions: Penalty task

**Variants:**
• Guest Voting: Audience guesses along
• Drinking Version: Different answer = both drink
• Story Version: Tell a story with each answer

**Tips:**
Mix funny ('Who snores louder?') with romantic questions ('Who said I love you first?'). Perfect for weddings!

mr_mrs_quiz.name: Mr & Mrs Quiz
mr_mrs_quiz.instructions:
**Preparation:**
Create 15-20 questions about the couple. Both partners get signs with 'Him' and 'Her' (or names). Questions pre-agreed with one partner secretly.

**Gameplay:**
The couple sits next to each other. For each question, both hold up the sign for whom the answer applies. The 'correct' answer was predetermined.

**Rules:**
• Show simultaneously (no cheating!)
• Correct answer = Point
• Wrong answer = Penalty task or shot
• Most points wins

**Variants:**
• Guest Edition: Guests guess along
• Relationship Timeline: Questions chronological
• Video Version: Answers were pre-recorded

**Tips:**
Mix embarrassing to romantic questions. The best questions reveal sweet or funny stories. Moderator should follow up!

newspaper_dance.name: Newspaper Dance
newspaper_dance.description: The dance floor gets smaller and smaller!
newspaper_dance.instructions:
**Preparation:**
Each couple gets an unfolded newspaper as their dance floor. Prepare music. Clear space for dancing.

**Gameplay:**
All couples dance on their newspaper. After each round, the newspaper is folded (halved). Whoever steps off the newspaper or falls is eliminated.

**Rules:**
• Both partners must stay on the newspaper
• Lifting partner is allowed!
• Newspaper may not be torn
• Music stops = Fold

**Variants:**
• Freeze Dance: Freeze when music stops
• Balloon Dance: Additionally balloon between bodies
• Speed Version: Fold newspaper every 30 seconds

**Tips:**
The secret: Put partner on your feet or balance on one leg! Perfect for weddings and parties.

wedding_bingo.name: Wedding Bingo
wedding_bingo.instructions:
**Preparation:**
Create bingo cards with typical wedding scenes: 'Someone cries', 'Father of bride speech', 'Child runs through frame', 'Grandma dances'. Each guest gets a card.

**Gameplay:**
During the wedding, guests cross off observed scenes. Whoever completes a row calls 'Wedding Bingo!' and wins a prize.

**Rules:**
• Scenes must actually happen
• Intentional triggering doesn't count
• Row: horizontal, vertical, or diagonal
• Multiple winners possible

**Variants:**
• Full House: All squares for main prize
• Team Bingo: Tables play against each other
• Photo Bingo: Scenes must be photographed

**Tips:**
Cards should be different! Mix inevitable ('Cutting the cake') with rare moments ('Someone spills wine').

polaroid_guestbook.name: Polaroid Guestbook
polaroid_guestbook.instructions:
**Preparation:**
Provide Polaroid camera with enough film. Guestbook/album with blank pages. Pens, glue, and decorations. Props for fun photos.

**Gameplay:**
Guests take Polaroid photos (individuals, couples, groups). Photos are glued into the guestbook and accompanied by personal greetings.

**Rules:**
• Max. 2 photos per guest (film is expensive!)
• At least one greeting per photo
• Use props box
• Let photos dry before gluing

**Variants:**
• Photo Booth: Fixed station with background
• Task Photos: Recreate specific poses
• Video Guestbook: Additionally short videos

**Tips:**
A 'guestbook attendant' helps immensely! Instax cameras are cheaper than real Polaroids. Washi tape instead of glue for nicer decoration!

ring_warming.name: Ring Warming
ring_warming.instructions:
**Preparation:**
The wedding rings are placed on a ribbon or in a small pouch. During the ceremony or reception, it's passed through all guests.

**Gameplay:**
Each guest briefly holds the rings in their hand and silently gives them good wishes, blessings, or positive energy. Then they're passed on.

**Rules:**
• Silent wishes (don't speak aloud)
• Only briefly each (10-30 seconds)
• Don't open/try on the ring
• At ceremony: Rings back to best man

**Variants:**
• Wish Cards: Additionally written wishes
• Blessing Round: Everyone speaks a wish aloud
• Photo Moment: Everyone photographed with ring

**Tips:**
For large weddings, start in multiple rows simultaneously. A helper keeps track. Very emotional moment!

anniversary_dance.name: Anniversary Dance
anniversary_dance.description: Married couples on the dance floor by years.
anniversary_dance.instructions:
**Preparation:**
DJ or moderator prepares a slow song. All married couples are invited to the dance floor.

**Gameplay:**
All couples begin dancing. The moderator asks couples to sit down: 'Those married less than 1 year...' then 5 years, 10, 20, etc. The longest-married couple remains and gives advice.

**Rules:**
• Honest answers!
• Only legally married couples
• The last couple gets a prize
• Often: Short speech or advice for the newlyweds

**Variants:**
• Relationship Dance: All couples, not just married
• Countdown: From long marriage to short
• Interview: Winning couple is interviewed

**Tips:**
Very emotional moment! Often grandparents are the winners. Have tissues ready. Perfect moment for photos!

kiss_marathon.name: Kiss Marathon
kiss_marathon.description: The longest kiss of the evening!
kiss_marathon.instructions:
**Preparation:**
Prepare stopwatch. Guests gather. Optional: Music and cheering. The couple stands in the center.

**Gameplay:**
On command, the couple begins kissing. Guests count the seconds or a moderator times it. Goal: Kiss as long as possible!

**Rules:**
• Lips may not separate
• Breathing through nose is allowed
• No breaking due to laughter
• Guests may cheer

**Variants:**
• Couple Challenge: Multiple couples compete
• Surprise Kiss: Guests call 'Kiss!' when glasses clink
• Break Record: Beat previous record

**Tips:**
Cheering makes it harder (laughing!). World record is over 58 hours - that's not necessary! 1-2 minutes is impressive.

bride_stealing.name: Bride Stealing
bride_stealing.description: The bride is 'kidnapped' and must be ransomed.
bride_stealing.instructions:
**Preparation:**
A group of guests secretly plans the 'kidnapping'. Target location (nearby bar) is scouted. Ransom tasks are prepared.

**Gameplay:**
The bride is 'kidnapped' during the celebration and taken to a bar. The groom must find her and ransom her through tasks or 'payment'.

**Rules:**
• Bride must be informed or consent
• Kidnapping not too early (after dinner)
• Ransom: Tasks, not just money
• Duration: Max. 1-2 hours

**Variants:**
• Bar Rally: Multiple bars as stations
• Task Ransom: Each task = one step closer
• Team Rescue: Groom + groomsmen

**Tips:**
All guests should know what's happening. The bride should really consent! Best coordinated with restaurant/DJ.

charades.name: Charades
charades.instructions:
**Preparation:**
Write terms on slips (movies, celebrities, actions). Form teams (3-6 people). Prepare timer (60-90 seconds per round).

**Gameplay:**
One player draws a term and acts it out - no words, sounds, or pointing at objects. The team guesses. On hit: Next term.

**Rules:**
• No speaking, humming, or sounds
• Don't point at objects
• Showing syllables with fingers allowed
• Time per round: 60-90 seconds
• Points per guessed term

**Variants:**
• Themed Charades: Only movies, only professions
• Speed Charades: 30 seconds per term
• Reverse: Everyone mimes, one guesses

**Tips:**
Learn common gestures: 'Movie' (crank camera), 'Book' (open hands), 'Song' (mime singing). The crazier the performance, the funnier!

activity.name: Activity
activity.description: Explain, draw, or act out - versatile party game.
activity.instructions:
**Preparation:**
Terms on cards (or buy Activity game). Form teams. Provide drawing materials. Timer (30-60 seconds).

**Gameplay:**
Depending on the card, the term must be explained (without related words), drawn, or acted out. The team guesses within time.

**Rules:**
• Explaining: Don't say the word
• Drawing: No letters or numbers
• Pantomime: No sounds
• Per hit: 1 point
• Wrong method: 0 points

**Variants:**
• Speed Mode: All methods in one round
• Bachelor Party Edition: Only relationship terms
• Drinking Activity: Wrong guess = Drink

**Tips:**
Mix easy and difficult terms. The funniest moments come from difficult pantomimes. Use a large drawing surface!

taboo.name: Taboo
taboo.description: Explain the term without using the forbidden words.
taboo.instructions:
**Preparation:**
Prepare Taboo cards: Each card has a term and 5 forbidden words below. Form teams. Prepare timer.

**Gameplay:**
One player explains the term without using the taboo words or their word stems. An opponent monitors. The team guesses.

**Rules:**
• Taboo word said = Term lost
• No 'Rhymes with...' or spelling
• Opponent can call 'Taboo!'
• 60 seconds per round
• Points per guessed term

**Variants:**
• Expert Mode: 7 taboo words
• Speed Taboo: 30 seconds
• Themed Rounds: Only movies, only sports

**Tips:**
Circumlocutions and stories work well. 'Imagine...' is a great opener. The taboo words are the obvious ones - be creative!

cards_against.name: Cards Against Humanity
cards_against.description: The card game for horrible people.
cards_against.instructions:
**Preparation:**
Get card deck (or print online). Each player receives 10 white cards. Black cards as a pile in the middle.

**Gameplay:**
A 'Card Czar' draws a black card and reads it aloud. Everyone else plays a white card that fits best. The Czar chooses the winner.

**Rules:**
• The Czar changes each round
• White cards played anonymously
• Winner keeps the black card
• For __ & __, play two white cards
• Czar's decision is final!

**Variants:**
• Drinking Version: Everyone except winner drinks
• Democracy: Everyone votes
• Haiku: Three cards into a story

**Tips:**
The Czar determines the humor! Some like clever, others love shocking. Play with people who understand dark humor.

scavenger_hunt.name: Scavenger Hunt
scavenger_hunt.instructions:
**Preparation:**
Plan 8-15 stations with clues. Each clue leads to the next location. At the end, a 'treasure' awaits. Form teams or play together.

**Gameplay:**
Teams start with the first clue. Each clue is a puzzle that must be solved to find the next location. Until the goal!

**Rules:**
• Follow the order
• No help from outsiders
• All team members must be present
• Photos at each station as proof
• First team at goal wins

**Variants:**
• Photo Hunt: Photo tasks instead of locations
• QR Code Hunt: Digital clues
• Night Hunt: With flashlights

**Tips:**
Clues should be tricky but solvable. A mix of knowledge, searching, and creativity. The 'treasure' can be snacks, drinks, or a surprise!

murder_mystery.name: Murder Mystery Dinner
murder_mystery.description: Solve the murder case while you dine.
murder_mystery.instructions:
**Preparation:**
Buy Murder Mystery set or create your own. Distribute roles to guests (in advance!). Decoration matching the theme. Plan dinner.

**Gameplay:**
During a multi-course dinner, everyone plays their role. Clues are revealed, suspects questioned. At the end, the murderer must be unmasked.

**Rules:**
• Everyone stays in character
• Murderer may lie, others may not
• Clues given at specific times
• Discussion and questioning allowed
• At the end: Vote

**Variants:**
• 1920s Gatsby Murder
• Crime Dinner at restaurant
• Outdoor: Murder at the castle

**Tips:**
Distribute roles in advance so guests can prepare. Costumes make it more authentic. Plan 3-4 hours!

kubb.name: Viking Chess
kubb.description: The Scandinavian throwing game for all ages.
kubb.instructions:
**Preparation:**
Mark playing field (5x8 meters). Set up five Kubbs per side. King in the middle. Distribute throwing sticks (6 pieces per team).

**Gameplay:**
Teams take turns throwing at the opponent's Kubbs. Knocked-over Kubbs are thrown into the opponent's field and set up there. Goal: Hit all Kubbs, then the King.

**Rules:**
• Throw sticks underhand (helicopter forbidden)
• First hit all field Kubbs, then base Kubbs
• King hit too early = lost!
• Throwing stick must land upright

**Variants:**
• Speed Kubb: Time limit per round
• Drinking Kubb: Hit Kubb = Opponent drinks
• Team Tournament: Multiple teams

**Tips:**
Strategy is important: Throw Kubbs close together for chain reaction. Be careful with the King - many games were lost this way!

sack_race.name: Sack Race
sack_race.description: The classic for every family celebration.
sack_race.instructions:
**Preparation:**
Potato sacks or large trash bags for each participant. Mark start and finish lines (10-20 meters). Obstacles optional.

**Gameplay:**
All participants get into the sacks and hold them with both hands. On command, hop to the finish. First to arrive wins.

**Rules:**
• Hands must stay on the sack
• Fall = Get up and continue
• Sack may not be torn
• No pushing
• Crossing finish line counts

**Variants:**
• Relay Sack Race: Teams with handover
• Obstacle Course: With slalom
• Pair Sack Race: Two people, one sack

**Tips:**
Smaller hops for balance rather than big jumps. Play on soft ground (grass). Start children and adults separately for fairness!

egg_and_spoon.name: Egg and Spoon Race
egg_and_spoon.description: Balance the egg on the spoon to the finish.
egg_and_spoon.instructions:
**Preparation:**
For each participant: A spoon and a raw egg (or hard-boiled/plastic). Mark start and finish lines (15-20 meters).

**Gameplay:**
The egg sits on the spoon, held by one hand at the handle. On command, everyone runs to the finish. If the egg drops: Back to start.

**Rules:**
• Only one hand on the spoon
• Other hand behind the back
• Stabilizing egg with hand = Disqualification
• Dropped egg: Back to start
• First intact egg at finish wins

**Variants:**
• Relay: Teams with handover
• Obstacle Course: With curves and hurdles
• Water Egg: Spoon with water instead of egg

**Tips:**
Slow and steady is better than fast! Don't stare at the egg - look ahead. Hard-boiled eggs for less chaos!

musical_chairs.name: Musical Chairs
musical_chairs.description: When the music stops, sit down fast!
musical_chairs.instructions:
**Preparation:**
Set up chairs in a row or circle - always one fewer than players. Prepare music (with pause option).

**Gameplay:**
Everyone walks around the chairs while music plays. When music stops: Sit down quickly! Whoever doesn't get a chair is out. One chair is removed.

**Rules:**
• Don't touch or reserve before music stops
• Sit immediately when music stops
• No pushing
• Must sit on chair (not just touch)
• Last player with chair wins

**Variants:**
• Music Freeze: Freeze when stopped, don't sit
• Team Version: Pairs play together
• Dance-Off: Eliminated players keep dancing

**Tips:**
Music controller shouldn't watch! Unpredictable pauses make it more exciting. Set up chairs stably!

kiss_challenge.name: Kiss Challenge
kiss_challenge.description: Collect kisses from strangers - the more the better!
kiss_challenge.instructions:
**Preparation:**
Get white t-shirt for the guest of honor. Provide various colored lipsticks. Don't forget disinfecting wipes!

**Gameplay:**
The guest of honor must collect kisses (lipstick prints) from strangers on the t-shirt. The group accompanies and documents everything.

**Rules:**
• Only voluntary kisses count!
• Respectful asking is mandatory
• Different colors = Bonus
• No harassment!
• Time limit: 1-2 hours

**Variants:**
• Photo Kiss: Selfie with each person
• Signatures: Additionally collect names
• Challenge Escalation: Find specific 'types'

**Tips:**
Always ask friendly and respectfully. Accept 'no'! Bars and clubs work best. The t-shirt is a great souvenir!

pictionary.name: Pictionary
pictionary.description: Draw and guess terms against the clock.
pictionary.instructions: Draw the term so your team can guess it. No letters or symbols allowed!

family_feud.name: Family Feud
family_feud.description: Guess the most popular survey answers.
family_feud.instructions: Teams compete. For each question, guess the most popular answers. Points for hits!

two_truths_lie.name: Two Truths and a Lie
two_truths_lie.description: Guess the lie among three statements.
two_truths_lie.instructions: Everyone tells three facts about themselves - two true, one false. The group must guess the lie!

80s_retro.name: 80s Retro Party
80s_retro.description: Neon colors, synth music and shoulder pads
80s_retro.tips: Think neon lights, disco balls and 80s hits from A-ha to Madonna!

casino_royale.name: Casino Royale
casino_royale.description: Elegant James Bond atmosphere with gaming tables
casino_royale.tips: Red carpet, poker chips, gaming tables and elegant cocktails!

tropical_beach.name: Tropical Beach
tropical_beach.description: Caribbean flair with palm trees and cocktails
tropical_beach.tips: Hawaiian shirts, tiki torches, coconut drinks and tropical music!

gatsby_20s.name: Gatsby / 1920s
gatsby_20s.description: The roaring twenties with flappers and jazz
gatsby_20s.tips: Art deco decor, champagne, feather boas, swing music and Charleston!

mexican_fiesta.name: Mexican Fiesta
mexican_fiesta.tips: Sombreros, piñatas, colorful banners, margaritas and salsa music!

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: By using EventBliss, you agree to these terms. Please read them carefully. These Terms apply to customers (consumers under § 13 of the German Civil Code) who book event services from independent agencies via the EventBliss marketplace.

acceptance.title: Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.content: By accessing or using EventBliss, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. Each booking confirms that you have read and accepted these Terms.

services.title: Our Role as a Platform
services.content:
EventBliss operates an online marketplace where independent event agencies offer their services (e.g. DJs, photographers, catering, decoration, venues).

Important: EventBliss acts as an intermediary and is NOT the contractual provider of the booked service. The service contract is concluded exclusively between you (the customer) and the respective agency. EventBliss provides the technical infrastructure, payment processing, communication, support and dispute resolution.

booking_process.title: Booking Process & Contract Formation
booking_process.content:
1. Request: You choose an agency/service and submit a booking request with date, details and your contact information.

2. Confirmation: The contract with the agency is formed either (a) when the online payment via Stripe is successfully completed (automatic confirmation) or (b) when the agency manually confirms your request (for on-site payment bookings).

3. Performance: The agency delivers the service on the agreed date. EventBliss is not involved in the delivery itself but coordinates communication via the platform.

Right of withdrawal for distance contracts (§§ 312g, 355 BGB): As a consumer you generally have a 14-day right of withdrawal. Exception: for date-bound leisure services (§ 312g (2) No. 9 BGB) – which applies to virtually all event services with a fixed date – the statutory right of withdrawal does not apply. The service-specific cancellation policy (see Cancellation) applies additionally and is more customer-friendly.

All communication with the agency runs through the platform chat. Please keep communication there so it is documented in case of dispute.

payment_methods.title: Payment Methods
payment_methods.content:
EventBliss supports two payment methods. Availability is decided by each agency per service:

• Online payment (Stripe): Credit card (Visa, Mastercard, Amex), Apple Pay, Google Pay, SEPA direct debit and other country-specific methods. Payment is instant and secured by Stripe as a PCI-DSS-compliant payment service provider. You receive an automatic booking confirmation and receipt by email after successful payment.

• On-site payment (on_site): You pay the price directly to the agency on the event day (or as agreed) in cash or via an agreed method. The listed price is binding – the agency may not demand more than stated in the booking. The booking is confirmed by manual agency confirmation via the platform.

Total price & platform fee: All prices on EventBliss are final prices including applicable VAT (where the agency is VAT-registered) and the 10 % platform fee. This fee is settled internally between the agency and EventBliss; you only pay the displayed total price.

Payment issues: For problems with online payments (e.g. failed charges, chargebacks) or on-site payments (e.g. price deviations) contact support@event-bliss.com. EventBliss mediates in either case – including for on-site bookings.

payment.title: Payment Terms & Settlement
payment.content:
For online payments, the total amount is charged immediately upon booking. Release to the agency occurs after service delivery according to the payout schedule (typically 24–72 hours after the event) to allow refunds in case of disputes.

For on-site payments, no amount is charged in advance. Payment is made directly to the agency.

Receipts & invoices: For online payments you automatically receive a Stripe payment confirmation and – if provided by the agency – an invoice in your booking area. For on-site payments, the agency is required to issue a receipt/invoice.

Default interest and further costs for late payment follow the statutory rules (§§ 286, 288 BGB).

cancellation_refund.title: Cancellation & Refunds
cancellation_refund.content:
Each agency sets a cancellation policy per service. It is transparently shown before you complete a booking. Three tiers exist:

• flexible: Full refund up to 7 days before the event, 50 % thereafter.
• moderate: Full refund up to 14 days before the event, 50 % until 7 days, none thereafter.
• strict: 50 % refund up to 30 days before the event, none thereafter.

Refund method: For online payments, refunds are automatically returned to your original payment method. Depending on your bank or card issuer, the credit appears within 5 to 14 days.

On-site bookings: Since no money has flowed through EventBliss, cancellations are settled directly with the agency. The agreed cancellation policy still applies. In case of disagreement EventBliss mediates; contact support@event-bliss.com with your booking number.

Force majeure & special cases: If the agency is unable to perform (e.g. illness, official restrictions), the full amount is refunded regardless of the cancellation tier.

Agency-caused non-performance: If the agency fails to show up or delivers grossly defective service, EventBliss buyer protection applies. Report the incident within 48 hours of the scheduled date with evidence to support@event-bliss.com – if substantiated, you receive a full refund.

off_platform_bypass.title: Platform Bypass Protection
off_platform_bypass.content:
Agencies on EventBliss are contractually obliged to process ALL bookings that originated on the platform through the platform. This protects you as a customer and ensures you receive all buyer protection benefits.

What is NOT allowed: An agency may NOT contact you – particularly after a cancellation, but also during initial contact – to arrange a booking off-platform (e.g. via WhatsApp, phone, email, cash payment, direct bank transfer) to avoid the platform fee. Such attempts are a breach of contract by the agency toward EventBliss.

Why you should refuse such offers: By going off-platform you lose all EventBliss protections:

• No buyer protection: No refund in case of non-performance, damage or fraud.
• No support: EventBliss cannot help you because no contract runs through the platform.
• No documented communication: No traceable evidence in case of dispute.
• No review & identity verification: The platform can no longer vouch for reliability.
• Tax concerns: Cash payment without a proper invoice can cause trouble for both sides.

Reporting bypass attempts: If an agency contacts you directly and offers you an off-platform booking (e.g. "Pay me 200 € in cash and we'll save the fee"), please report it immediately to compliance@event-bliss.com. Helpful items: screenshot of the message, booking/service ID, time of contact.

Thank-you credit: As a token of appreciation, after verification we will credit you with 20 % off your next EventBliss booking (max. 200 € value, redeemable within 12 months).

Confidentiality: Your report is treated confidentially. The reported agency will not learn who submitted the report.

prohibited.title: Prohibited Activities
prohibited.content:
You agree not to use the platform for illegal, fraudulent or harmful purposes. In particular, the following is prohibited:

• Misuse of payment methods (e.g. unjustified chargebacks after the service was rendered online, refusal to pay for on-site bookings).
• Abusive, discriminatory or threatening behavior toward agencies or staff.
• Review manipulation (e.g. fake reviews, blackmailing with negative reviews).
• Providing false information in bookings (e.g. false identity, fraudulent payment methods).
• Using the platform to broker illegal content, services or products.
• Bypassing the platform yourself after initial contact via EventBliss (see "Platform Bypass Protection").

liability.title: Limitation of Liability
liability.content:
As a platform operator, EventBliss is liable only for damages resulting from intent or gross negligence, as well as for injury to life, body and health according to statutory provisions.

The quality, timeliness and contractual conformity of the booked service are the sole responsibility of the respective agency – EventBliss is not the service provider.

However, EventBliss voluntarily grants buyer protection on online payments (see Cancellation & Refunds) and mediates in case of disputes, also for on-site bookings.

changes.title: Changes to Terms
changes.content: We may update these Terms to reflect legal changes or new features. Material changes will be announced to you by email at least 30 days before they take effect. Continued use after they take effect constitutes acceptance.

governingLaw.title: Governing Law & Jurisdiction
governingLaw.content:
"EventBliss" is a brand and product of MYFAMBLISS GROUP LTD, Gladstonos 12-14, 8046 Paphos, Cyprus. These Terms are governed by the law of the Republic of Cyprus, excluding the UN Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods. For consumers habitually resident in the European Union, this choice of law applies only to the extent it does not deprive the consumer of the protection of mandatory consumer-protection provisions of the state of their habitual residence (Article 6(2) Rome I Regulation). Mandatory consumer rights — in particular the right of withdrawal under § 312g German Civil Code for consumers resident in Germany — remain unaffected.

Jurisdiction: For consumers, the statutory rules on jurisdiction apply, in particular the consumer's place of residence. Against entrepreneurs, exclusive jurisdiction lies at the registered seat of MYFAMBLISS GROUP LTD in the Republic of Cyprus.

Out-of-court dispute resolution: The EU Commission provides an online dispute resolution platform: https://ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr. We are neither obliged nor willing to participate in dispute resolution proceedings before a consumer arbitration board, but strive for direct resolution via support@event-bliss.com.

agency_agreement.title: Agency Agreement (EventBliss Partner Agreement)
agency_agreement.intro: "EventBliss" is a brand and product of MYFAMBLISS GROUP LTD, a company incorporated under the laws of the Republic of Cyprus, registered in the Cyprus Companies Register, with registered office at Gladstonos 12-14, 8046 Paphos, Cyprus. This Agency Agreement governs the legal relationship between MYFAMBLISS GROUP LTD as operator of the EventBliss platform and the agencies offering event services through EventBliss. The binding version of this agreement is the German version; this English translation is provided for convenience only.
agency_agreement.parties: Parties: MYFAMBLISS GROUP LTD, Gladstonos 12-14, 8046 Paphos, Cyprus, registered in the Cyprus Companies Register (the "Company"; the platform operated under the brand "EventBliss" is hereinafter referred to as "EventBliss" or the "Platform"), and the registered event services agency (the "Agency"). A "Customer" is any natural or legal person booking through the Platform.

preamble.title: Preamble and Scope
preamble.content: EventBliss operates an online marketplace matching customers with event service providers. This agreement applies to all legal relationships between EventBliss and the Agency using the Platform. Definitions for Platform, Agency, Customer, Booking, and Referred Customer apply as set out in the German version.

platformServices.title: Platform Services
platformServices.content: EventBliss provides public listings, a digital booking workflow, payment processing via Stripe for online bookings, support services, and marketing. EventBliss acts solely as an intermediary and is not a party to the service contract between Agency and Customer.

agencyDuties.title: Agency Obligations
agencyDuties.content: The Agency must provide services with proper quality, comply with all legal and tax obligations, keep profile data and calendar accurate, respond to inquiries within 24 hours on business days, and display legally binding prices including VAT.

platformFee.title: Platform Fee
platformFee.content: EventBliss charges a 10% platform fee on all bookings, regardless of payment method. For online bookings, the fee is deducted automatically before payout. For on-site payments, the Agency must remit the fee within 14 days after service delivery. Each completed on-site booking must be marked as delivered within 48 hours.

paymentModes.title: Payment Modes and Equal Treatment
paymentModes.content: Both online (Stripe) and on-site payments are recognized as Platform bookings. The choice of payment method does not exempt the Agency from the platform fee. Encouraging customers to pay outside the Platform is prohibited and constitutes a breach of Section 6.

exclusivity.title: EXCLUSIVITY AND ANTI-CIRCUMVENTION
exclusivity.leadIn: Breach of this Section 6 entitles EventBliss to immediate termination, contractual penalties, and suspension of the Agency profile.

period.title: Exclusivity Period
period.content: Any customer referred via the Platform is exclusive to EventBliss for 18 months from first contact, including follow-up bookings and related derivative business.

prohibition.title: Prohibition of Circumvention
prohibition.content: After cancellation, the Agency may not offer a comparable service off-platform. The Agency may not encourage cash payment on site when the listing is set to online payment, share customer contacts with third parties, run parallel bookings via own channels, or induce customers to cancel platform bookings in favor of off-platform arrangements.

dataPurpose.title: Purpose Limitation of Customer Data
dataPurpose.content: Customer contact data received from the Platform is subject to strict purpose limitation per GDPR Art. 5(1)(b). Use is limited to fulfilling the specific booking; building own customer lists, marketing, newsletters, and upselling beyond the booking are prohibited.

penalty.title: Contractual Penalty
penalty.content: For each proven violation of Sections 6.1 to 6.3, the Agency owes a contractual penalty equal to 2× the total booking value (minimum EUR 500) plus a lump sum of EUR 10,000 per incident, payable without further notice. Additional damages remain reserved.

audit.title: Audit Right on Reasonable Suspicion
audit.content: Upon reasonable suspicion, EventBliss may request communications logs, payment records, and booking calendar extracts within 14 days. Refusal or late delivery raises a rebuttable presumption of breach.

cancellationRates.title: Cancellation Rate Rules
cancellationRates.leadIn: Excessive agency-initiated cancellations harm the Platform. EventBliss continuously monitors cancellation rates.

window.title: Reference Window
window.content: The rate is calculated on a rolling 30-day window, counting only agency-initiated cancellations relative to confirmed bookings.

warning.title: Early Warning at 15%
warning.content: Above 15%, the Agency is warned automatically and must attend a mandatory partner management call within 14 days.

suspension.title: Suspension and Delisting at 20%
suspension.content: Above 20%, EventBliss may — after 7 days' written notice — pause listings, pause the entire profile, or remove the Agency from the Platform. Measures lift once the rate falls below 15%.

exceptions.title: Exceptions with Evidence
exceptions.content: Force majeure, customer misinformation, customer payment default, and legitimate safety or hygiene concerns are excluded from the calculation if timely documented and supported by evidence.

qualityCriteria.title: Listing and Quality Criteria
qualityCriteria.content: Response time under 24 hours on business days, minimum average rating of 4.0/5.0 over the last 20 bookings, complete profiles, legally binding prices per PAngV, and valid usage rights for all imagery.

liability.title: Liability and Warranty
liability.content: The Agency is solely liable for service delivery and incidents at the venue. EventBliss is liable only for platform availability within market standards. The Agency indemnifies EventBliss against third-party claims arising from defective service delivery and must maintain business liability insurance of at least EUR 1,000,000 (personal injury) and EUR 500,000 (property damage).

dataProtection.title: Data Protection and Processing
dataProtection.content: Each party acts as an independent controller for its own processing purposes. Where EventBliss processes data on behalf of the Agency, the separate Data Processing Agreement under GDPR Art. 28 applies. Data breaches involving platform customers must be reported to EventBliss within 24 hours of discovery.

termTermination.title: Term and Termination
termTermination.content: The agreement runs indefinitely. Either party may terminate for convenience with 30 days' written notice to month's end. EventBliss may terminate immediately for breaches of Section 6, repeated cancellation rate violations, substantial payment default, or insolvency. The 18-month exclusivity in Section 6.1 survives termination.

finalProvisions.title: Final Provisions
finalProvisions.content:
Governing law: This agreement is governed exclusively by the laws of the Republic of Cyprus, excluding the UN Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods (CISG) and any conflict-of-laws rules that would lead to the application of another jurisdiction. Mandatory consumer-protection rules of the customer's habitual residence remain unaffected between Agency and Customer.

Exclusive jurisdiction: For all disputes arising out of or in connection with this agreement, the competent courts at the seat of MYFAMBLISS GROUP LTD in the Republic of Cyprus have exclusive jurisdiction, to the extent permitted by law. EventBliss reserves the right to sue the Agency at its general place of business.

Alternative dispute resolution: Before initiating legal proceedings, the parties shall attempt an amicable resolution. Upon request, a structured mediation procedure shall be conducted under the rules of a recognised Cyprus-based institution.

Severability, form requirements, amendment rights, and assignment restrictions apply as set out in the German version. The binding version is the German original.

compliance.title: Report Suspected Circumvention
compliance.content: Customers, agencies, and third parties may report suspected violations confidentially. Whistleblower identity is protected on request.

bomb.title: Ticking Bomb
bomb.tagline: Answer questions before the bomb explodes — pure adrenaline!
bomb.step1: The bomb ticks and gets passed to a player
bomb.step2: Answer the question correctly before time runs out
bomb.step3: Wrong answer or too slow? BOOM — you're out!
bomb.step4: Last player standing wins the round
bomb.tip: Don't overthink it — the bomb waits for no one!

taboo.title: Taboo
taboo.tagline: Describe words — but watch out, certain words are absolutely TABOO!
taboo.step1: You see a word and 5 forbidden words
taboo.step2: Describe the word to your team — WITHOUT using the taboo words
taboo.step3: Your team must guess before time runs out
taboo.step4: Each correct guess earns a point
taboo.tip: Creativity beats logic — think outside the box!

headup.title: Heads Up
headup.tagline: Phone on forehead, friends describe, you guess — sounds easy? Think again!
headup.step1: Hold your phone to your forehead — you can't see the word
headup.step2: Your friends describe the word to you
headup.step3: Guessed right? Tilt phone forward! Wrong? Tilt back!
headup.step4: Get as many words as possible before time's up
headup.tip: Don't drop your phone — happens more often than you'd think!

category.title: Speed Category
category.tagline: Name things in a category — hesitate and you're out! Speed counts!
category.step1: A category is shown (e.g., "Animals starting with A")
category.step2: Each player takes turns naming a matching item
category.step3: Can't think of one or too slow? You get a point

emojiguess.title: Emoji Guess
emojiguess.tagline: Decode emojis, guess the answer — looks easy but it's not!
emojiguess.step1: You're shown emojis that represent something
emojiguess.step2: Guess which movie, song, or celeb the emojis show
emojiguess.step3: The faster you guess, the more points you get
emojiguess.tip: Think blockbusters — the obvious answer is often right!

fakeorfact.title: Fake or Fact
fakeorfact.tagline: Is that really true? Spot the fake facts — trust nothing!
fakeorfact.step1: A statement is shown to you
fakeorfact.step2: Decide: Is it TRUE or FAKE?
fakeorfact.step3: After your answer, you'll see the explanation
fakeorfact.tip: The craziest facts are usually true!

truthdare.title: Truth or Dare
truthdare.tagline: The party classic — answer honestly or do an embarrassing dare? Your choice!
truthdare.step2: Truth: Answer the question honestly (no matter how embarrassing!)
truthdare.step4: Then it's the next player's turn
truthdare.tip: Dares get bolder with every drink — we didn't say that!

thisorthat.title: This or That
thisorthat.tagline: Pizza or Pasta? Netflix or Cinema? Tough choices ahead!
thisorthat.step3: Compare answers — who's in the majority?
thisorthat.tip: There are no wrong answers — just heated debates!

whoami.title: Who Am I?
whoami.tagline: Ask yes/no questions and figure out who you are — Sherlock would be proud!
whoami.step1: Each player gets a secret person/character assigned
whoami.step2: Ask yes/no questions: "Am I real?" "Am I famous?"
whoami.step3: Combine the clues and guess who you are
whoami.tip: Start broad — "Am I a woman?" eliminates 50%!

bottlespin.title: Spin the Bottle
bottlespin.tagline: The bottle spins... who will it point to? Fun and suspense guaranteed!
bottlespin.step2: Whoever it points to must answer a question or do a task
bottlespin.tip: Luck decides — no tactics can help you here!

storybuilder.title: Story Builder
storybuilder.tagline: Write the craziest story together — creativity without limits!
storybuilder.step1: A story starter sets the scene
storybuilder.step2: Each player adds one sentence in turns
storybuilder.step3: At the end, the complete story is read aloud
storybuilder.tip: The more absurd your twist, the funnier the story!

sharedquiz.title: Shared Quiz
sharedquiz.tagline: Team quiz battle — who knows more? Show what you've got!
sharedquiz.step1: A question is shown to all players at once
sharedquiz.step2: Everyone picks their answer — no conferring!
sharedquiz.tip: Sometimes the most unlikely answer is the right one!

quickdraw.title: Quick Draw
quickdraw.tagline: Draw fast, guess faster — Picasso would be proud (or horrified)!
quickdraw.step1: You're shown a word to draw
quickdraw.step2: Draw as fast as possible — stick figures are fine!
quickdraw.step4: Faster guesses = more points for everyone
quickdraw.tip: Forget art class — speed is everything here!

splitquiz.title: Split Quiz
splitquiz.tagline: Split knowledge — everyone sees different answers, but only one is right!
splitquiz.step2: Each player sees different answer options
splitquiz.step3: Discuss which answer could be correct
splitquiz.step4: Only the one with the right answer scores
splitquiz.tip: Trust your gut — or the nerd in your group!

hochstapler.title: The Impostor
hochstapler.tagline: One person has no clue — find the impostor! Or be one yourself...
hochstapler.step1: All players get a secret word — ONE gets a different one
hochstapler.step2: Each player describes the word with a single term
hochstapler.step3: Everyone votes: Who is the impostor?
hochstapler.step4: Impostor caught? Others win. Impostor survives? They win!
hochstapler.tip: As the impostor: Be vague enough to blend in, but specific enough to not seem suspicious!

wo-ist-was.title: Where Is It?
wo-ist-was.tagline: 5 different modes — from Memory to Speed to Street View! Geography & observation!
wo-ist-was.step1: Choose one of 5 modes: Memory, Speed, Differences, Map or Street View
wo-ist-was.step2: Memory: Memorize the emoji scene, then answer questions about it
wo-ist-was.step3: Speed: The scene stays visible — who finds the answer fastest?
wo-ist-was.step4: Map: Find cities and countries on the world map — closer = more points
wo-ist-was.step5: Street View: You see a photo — guess which city you're in!
wo-ist-was.tip: In Map mode: Europe is smaller than you think — and Australia is bigger!

flaschendrehen.title: Spin the Bottle
flaschendrehen.tagline: The classic party game! Who will the bottle choose?
flaschendrehen.step1: The bottle spins in the middle
flaschendrehen.step2: Whoever it points to is up
flaschendrehen.step3: Answer the question or complete the dare
flaschendrehen.tip: Karma spins the bottle — be nice to your fellow players!

drueck-das-wort.title: Press the Word
drueck-das-wort.tagline: Find the right word under time pressure — one wrong tap and it gets tight!
drueck-das-wort.step2: Tap the correct word from the options — fast!
drueck-das-wort.step3: Correct? Next word! Wrong? Points deducted!
drueck-das-wort.step4: Most correct words at the end wins
drueck-das-wort.tip: Don't tap frantically — one wrong click costs more than a second of thinking!

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.q1: What happens if I miss the 50% discount offer?
faq.a1: You'll keep the price of €49 (Professional) or €149 (Enterprise) forever – even if we later raise it to €98/€298. Your rate gets locked at booking.
faq.a2: Yes. Upgrade is instant, downgrade at the end of the billing period. No notice periods, no cancellation fees.
faq.q3: How many users will I actually reach?
faq.a3: EventBliss grows via 3 viral loops (games, surveys, TV). With Professional you're in priority search – wedding couples, party hosts and companies find you where they plan.
faq.a4: Commission only applies to actually booked services, settled automatically via Stripe. Starter pays 25%, Professional & Enterprise only 10% – quickly offsets the monthly fee.
faq.q5: Do I need experience or setup help?
faq.a5: No. Profile + first service listing are live in under 10 minutes. Enterprise customers also get a dedicated onboarding call.
